title: Multiplayer Fishing Mini-Game
prompt:
Create a single HTML file with a multiplayer fishing mini-game using Three.js and GLTFLoader.

## Scene Setup
- Use a perspective camera at position (0, 12, 20) looking at (0, 0, 0).
- Add ambient light (intensity 0.6) and a directional light at (10, 15, 10) with intensity 0.8 and castShadow enabled.
- Create a water surface: a PlaneGeometry(40, 40) at y=0 with a semi-transparent blue MeshPhongMaterial (color #1a6ea0, opacity 0.7, transparent true). Rotate it to be horizontal.
- Create a ground/shore: a PlaneGeometry(40, 10) at z=22, y=-0.5 with green/brown material for the shore.
- Add a skybox or set scene background to a light sky blue color (#87CEEB).

## Fox Model (Mascot)
- Load 'Fox.glb' using GLTFLoader from CDN (https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/three@0.160.0/examples/jsm/loaders/GLTFLoader.js).
- Place the Fox at position (8, 0, 18) with scale (0.04, 0.04, 0.04), rotated to face the water.
- The Fox serves as a decorative mascot on the shore. If the model has animations, play the idle/survey animation.

## Players
- Player 1 (P1): A red capsule/cylinder mesh at position (-5, 0.5, 15), representing a fisher on the left side of the shore.
- Player 2 (P2): A blue capsule/cylinder mesh at position (5, 0.5, 15), representing a fisher on the right side.
- Each player has a fishing line: a Line geometry from their position extending toward the water. The line end (bobber) is a small sphere.
- Active player indicator: highlight the active player's mesh with emissive glow.

## Fish System
- Spawn 8 fish as small elongated ellipsoid meshes (various colors: gold, silver, green, orange) at random positions on the water plane (x: -15 to 15, y: -0.5, z: -15 to 10).
- Each fish has a 'size' property: random value between 1 and 5 (integer), which determines score points.
- Fish swim in random directions, changing direction every 2-3 seconds, speed ~2 units/sec. Fish stay within the water bounds.
- Store fish data in an array with {id, mesh, size, caught, caughtBy}.

## Game Mechanics
- Turn-based: Players alternate turns. P1 goes first.
- Each turn has 3 phases: AIM, CAST, REEL.
- **AIM phase**: The active player moves their bobber target with A/D keys (left/right on x-axis, clamped to -15..15). A targeting reticle (ring) shows on the water surface where the bobber will land. Press SPACE to confirm and move to CAST phase.
- **CAST phase**: Animate the bobber flying from the player to the target position over 1 second. Auto-transition to REEL phase after landing.
- **REEL phase**: Check if any uncaught fish is within 3 units of the bobber position. If yes, the closest fish is 'hooked'. The fish mesh moves to the bobber and both animate back to the player over 1.5 seconds. The fish is marked caught, score is added. If no fish nearby, display 'No catch!' for 1 second. Then switch to the other player's turn.
- Game ends when all fish are caught OR after 16 total turns (8 per player). Show a winner overlay with scores.

## HUD / UI
- Top-left: 'Player 1 Score: X' in red text.
- Top-right: 'Player 2 Score: X' in blue text.
- Top-center: 'Turn: X/16' and current phase text (AIM/CAST/REEL).
- Bottom-center: Instructions text that changes per phase.
- A 'Reset Game' button at bottom-right that resets all state.
- Winner overlay: a centered div with large text showing the winner, hidden until game ends.

## State Management
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ with:
- activePlayer: 1 or 2
- phase: 'AIM' | 'CAST' | 'REEL' | 'ENDED'
- turn: current turn number (1-16)
- p1Score: number
- p2Score: number
- bobberX: current bobber target x position for active player
- fishCount: total fish count (8)
- fishCaught: number of fish caught so far
- lastCatchSize: size of last caught fish (0 if miss)
- gameOver: boolean
- winner: 0 (none), 1, or 2 (or 'tie')
- foxLoaded: boolean (true when Fox.glb is loaded)
- fishPositions: array of {x, z, caught} for each fish
- reticleVisible: boolean (true during AIM phase)

Update __3D_STATE__ every frame in the animation loop.
physics_constraints: Fish must stay within water bounds (-15..15 x, -15..10 z). Bobber target clamped to x: -15..15. Catch detection radius is 3 units. Fish movement speed ~2 units/sec. Bobber cast animation takes ~1 second. Reel animation takes ~1.5 seconds. Scores must be non-negative integers. Turn counter must not exceed 16.
